footer.tagline: A diabetes care companion built by DataSpark Tech LLC — free where it matters, no ads, ever.
footer.rights: MyHealthyGlucose is a personal tracking tool — it is not a medical device and does not diagnose, treat, or replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

hero.title: Diabetes care, built around the food you eat.
hero.subtitle: Track glucose, HbA1c, blood pressure, meals and medication — around real, regional food. Free tools, no ads, ever.

shell.trackTitle: Track this automatically in the app
shell.trackBody: MyHealthyGlucose logs carbs, glucose, BP and meds around real food.

disclaimer.label: For education only.
disclaimer.body: These estimates are not medical advice and don't diagnose or treat any condition. Always talk to your doctor about your own numbers and treatment.

hub.title: Calculators worth bookmarking.
hub.subtitle: Fast, free, and no login required. Every tool works anonymously — nothing you enter is stored.
hub.imageCaption: Plan meals, count carbs, and understand your numbers — in a couple of taps.
hub.suggestPrefix: More tools are added regularly — have one in mind?

carb.title: Carb Calculator
carb.body: Search Indian, Western and staple foods and build a meal to see the running carb total.

bmi.title: BMI Calculator
bmi.body: Estimate your Body Mass Index with real context on what it means for diabetes risk.

a1c.title: A1C ↔ Average Glucose
a1c.body: Translate an HbA1c percentage into an everyday average glucose number, and back.

riskQuiz.title: Diabetes Risk Quiz
riskQuiz.body: Six quick questions on the risk factors clinicians look at — a prompt to see a doctor.

glycemicIndex.title: Glycemic Index & Load
glycemicIndex.body: See how fast a food raises blood sugar — and the glycemic load for a real serving size.

mealSimulator.title: Meal Glycemic Simulator
mealSimulator.body: Build a full meal and see its estimated glucose impact, with smart lower-GI swaps.

cgmAnalyzer.title: CGM Data Visualizer
cgmAnalyzer.body: Drop in a Dexcom/Libre CSV to see your glucose curve and time-in-range — 100% private.

carbTarget.title: Daily Carb & Fiber Target
carbTarget.body: Estimate your daily carbohydrate and fiber targets from your calorie intake.

postMealTarget.title: Post-Meal Target Finder
postMealTarget.body: See the general after-meal glucose targets and check a reading against them.

insulinRatio.title: Insulin-to-Carb Ratio
insulinRatio.body: Estimate a mealtime insulin dose from your carb ratio and correction factor.

waterIntake.title: Hydration Calculator
waterIntake.body: A daily water target based on your weight and activity level.

emergencyGuide.title: Emergency Guide
emergencyGuide.body: Clear, printable steps for high and low blood sugar emergencies.

a1c.title: Translate your A1C into everyday numbers.
a1c.intro: HbA1c reflects your average glucose over ~3 months. This converts between the two using the standard ADAG formula.

bmi.title: Body Mass Index — with real context.
bmi.intro: A quick estimate of where your weight sits, plus what it means for diabetes risk. No login, no data stored.

cgm.title: See your 14-day glucose curve — instantly, privately.
cgm.intro: Drop in the CSV export from your Dexcom, FreeStyle Libre, or Contour device and get an instant visual summary. Everything happens in your browser — your data never leaves your device or gets uploaded anywhere.

carbCalc.title: Count carbs around real food.
carbCalc.intro: Search common dishes — Indian, Western and everyday staples — and build a meal to see the running carb total.

carbTarget.title: How many carbs and how much fiber per day?
carbTarget.intro: A general starting estimate for daily carbohydrate and fiber, based on your calorie intake. Personal targets vary — use this as a conversation starter with your dietitian.

emergency.title: What to do in a blood sugar emergency.
emergency.intro: Quick, clear steps for hypoglycemia (too low) and hyperglycemia (too high). Read it now so it's familiar before you ever need it — and always follow your own care plan.

gi.title: How fast does a food raise blood sugar?
gi.intro: Glycemic index (GI) ranks how quickly a food raises glucose. Glycemic load (GL) adjusts that for a real serving size — often the more useful number.

insulinRatio.title: Estimate a mealtime insulin dose.
insulinRatio.intro: For people who dose insulin by carb counting. This is an educational estimator — your real ratios and doses must come from your care team.

mealSim.title: Build a meal, see its glucose impact.
mealSim.intro: Add foods to construct a full meal and get its estimated glycemic load, carbs, protein and fat — plus smart swaps to lower the glucose peak.

postMeal.title: Is my after-meal reading in range?
postMeal.intro: See the general post-meal glucose targets for different groups, and check a reading against them. These are educational reference points — your personal targets come from your doctor.

riskQuiz.title: A quick read on your risk factors.
riskQuiz.intro: Six questions, modeled on the risk factors clinicians look at. It's a prompt to talk to a doctor — not a diagnosis.

water.title: How much water should you drink?
water.intro: Staying hydrated matters for everyone, and especially when managing blood sugar. This gives a general daily target based on your weight and activity.
